gaps: The official public OpenAPI spec does not declare security schemes even though protected Airbyte deployments need Authorization headers for most operational endpoints.
recommendation: Ship as a read-only Airbyte Admin CLI in the cloud category, generated from Airbyte's official Public API spec and patched only to document/support optional Authorization headers.

novel_features.name: Source and destination inventory
novel_features.description: Lists Airbyte sources, with sibling destination and connection-detail commands for inspecting configured data movement.
novel_features.rationale: Sources and destinations show the edges of Airbyte data movement and are useful for incident response, migration review, and agent summaries.

novel_features.name: Sync job visibility
novel_features.description: Lists sync jobs with connection filters and pagination for troubleshooting recent ELT activity.
novel_features.rationale: Data engineers often need to inspect recent sync history without opening the UI.

novel_features.name: Connector catalog lookup
novel_features.description: Shows available source or destination connector definitions for a workspace or organization.
novel_features.rationale: Connector discovery helps engineers understand what sources and destinations a workspace can support.

novel_features.name: Workspace and access audit
novel_features.description: Surfaces workspaces, users, organizations, permissions, and tags for Airbyte operational review.
novel_features.rationale: Workspace and access metadata lets agents answer governance and ownership questions without changing Airbyte state.

novel_features.name: Agent-ready local archive
novel_features.description: Caches Airbyte read endpoints locally for offline search, export, and repeatable agent analysis.
novel_features.rationale: Local archival makes repeated agent analysis cheaper, faster, and less dependent on live API availability.
